text: Git branching is an essential part of modern version control workflows. Instead of all developers working directly on the main codebase, they create separate feature branches for each new feature or bug fix. This keeps the main branch stable and clean. Once a developer finishes their work on a feature branch, they can request a code review by opening a pull request. Teammates can then suggest changes or approve the new code. After approval, the feature branch is merged back into the main branch. This process helps teams collaborate more effectively and avoid conflicts when building software.
